title: acinos arvensis subsp. eglandulosus
intro: Acinos arvensis subsp. eglandulosus, also known as lesser knapweed, is a subspecies of perennial herb that belongs to the mint family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Acinos arvensis subsp. eglandulosus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve respiratory problems, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
